## Overview

**North Star: The Drydock Blueprint**

A drydock blueprint is not decorative — it is an authoritative technical document. Every line is measured. Every annotation earns its place. Whitespace is not empty: it is tolerance, clearance, specification. The BLRT Grupp interface operates on this principle: grid-registered, dimensioned, readable at a glance by someone who has been at sea.

The palette is Hull Black, Navigation Steel, Signal Red. The surface is dark like a steel plate at rest, not dark like a hacker terminal. Depth is tonal — you move from hull to surface to card, each step a few degrees lighter, the way a technical cross-section reveals internal structure. No decorative shadows. Borders are functional, not decorative.

Typography is Outfit: a modern geometric sans that reads as engineered, not corporate. Headings are tight-tracked, body is measured.

**Persona:** A port procurement officer who has reviewed three drydock proposals this week. They scan quickly, read carefully when something earns it, and distrust any interface that tries too hard to impress.

---

## Colors

### Palette

| Token | Name | Hex | HSL | Usage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `--background` | Hull Black | `#0d121c` | 218 35% 8% | Page background |
| `--surface` | Hull Black Mid | `#131a25` | 218 32% 11% | Section backgrounds, input fills |
| `--card` | Hull Black Light | `#151c28` | 218 30% 12% | Card backgrounds |
| `--primary-deep` | Deep Navy | `#071327` | 218 68% 9% | Hero overlays, dark featured cells |
| `--foreground` | Navigation Steel Light | `#eaecf0` | 220 18% 93% | Body text, headings |
| `--muted-foreground` | Navigation Steel | `#909aad` | 218 15% 62% | Secondary text, labels |
| `--primary` | Survey Blue | `#739ee8` | 218 72% 68% | Links, interactive text, badges |
| `--brand-red` | Signal Red | `#ec483c` | 4 82% 58% | Accent, icons, active states, CTAs |
| `--border` | Steel Plate | `#252f41` | 218 28% 20% | Borders, dividers |
| `--muted` | Dark Steel | `#1f2633` | 218 25% 16% | Hover states, subtle fills |
| `--gold` | Brass | `#ffcc4d` | 43 100% 65% | Rare emphasis only — certifications, warnings |
| `--success` | Running Light Green | `#22c55e` | — | Status indicators (available, operational) |

### Color strategy: Committed

Signal Red (`--brand-red`) carries the active/interactive register across the entire UI — icon fills, active tab underlines, dot markers, CTA backgrounds. Survey Blue (`--primary`) is for text-level interaction only (links, active headings). The tonal background stack (Hull Black → Hull Black Mid → Hull Black Light) is the elevation system.

### Rules

- Never use `#000` or `#fff` raw — the tinted palette handles extremes.
- Brass (`--gold`) is reserved: use it for a single emphasis element per page at most.
- Signal Red on a dark background requires `text-brand-red` (58% lightness), not the legacy dark variant. Never use Signal Red as body text.
- `--primary` (Survey Blue) at 68% lightness is specifically calibrated to pass WCAG AA against Hull Black. Do not darken it for "safety" — it will become illegible.
- Success green is for status pulses only. Not decoration.

---

## Typography

**Family:** Outfit (Google Fonts) — geometric sans, single weight axis.

### Scale

| Role | Size | Weight | Tracking | Line height |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Hero H1 | 60px / 3.75rem | 700 | −0.04em (`tracking-tighter`) | 1.1 |
| Page H2 | 30px / 1.875rem | 700 | −0.03em (`tracking-tighter`) | 1.2 |
| Section H3 | 20px / 1.25rem | 600 | −0.02em (`tracking-tight`) | 1.3 |
| Card H4 | 16px / 1rem | 600 | −0.01em | 1.4 |
| Body | 15px / 0.9375rem | 400 | 0 | 1.6 (`leading-relaxed`) |
| Small / label | 12px / 0.75rem | 500–600 | +0.05–0.2em (`tracking-wide` to `tracking-[0.2em]`) | 1.5 |
| Overline | 10px / 0.625rem | 700 | +0.35em (`tracking-[0.35em]`) | 1 |

### Hierarchy rules

- Hero headings: `font-bold tracking-tighter` — tightest tracking at largest size. The blueprint aesthetic.
- Body max-width: 65ch (`max-w-[65ch]`). Never wider.
- Overline labels (section eyebrows): 10px, all-caps, `tracking-[0.2em]`, `text-muted-foreground` or `text-white/45` on dark. These are annotation text, not headings.
- Heading underline: `heading-underline` utility class (adds a 2px Signal Red left-aligned line below). Used on H2-level section heads only.
- Card headings: `tracking-tight` minimum — never fully loose on headings.
- Never set heading color to `text-primary` (Survey Blue) — headings are `text-foreground`.

---

## Elevation

**Model: Tonal layering**

Depth is communicated through the background step stack. No decorative shadows on cards or panels.

```
Hull Black (#0d121c)          ← Page background
  └─ Hull Black Mid (#131a25) ← Section backgrounds (bg-surface)
       └─ Hull Black Light (#151c28) ← Card surfaces (bg-card)
            └─ Deep Navy (#071327) ← Featured / hero panels (primary-deep)
```

### Shadows (functional only)

Shadows exist only for overlapping elements (dropdowns, modals, tooltips) and the Signal Red glow on primary CTAs. They are not decorative.

| Token | Value | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `--shadow-card` | `0 1px 3px 0 black/0.4, 0 1px 2px -1px black/0.4` | Card hover lift |
| `--shadow-elevated` | `0 16px 40px -12px black/0.65` | Modals, popovers |
| `--shadow-red` | `0 4px 24px -4px hsl(4 82% 40% / 0.55)` | CTA buttons with brand red |

### Border as separator

`border border-border` (`--border: #252f41`) is the primary visual separator. `gap-px bg-border` creates pixel-thin grid lines between cells — preferred over individual borders for grid layouts.

---

## Components

### Hero section

```
<section relative py-32 overflow-hidden>
  [image: absolute inset-0, w-full h-full object-cover, filter: saturate(0.65) brightness(0.22)]
  [gradient overlay: from primary-deep → navy/0.88 → navy/0.5]
  [fade to background: gradient-to-t from-background via-transparent]
  [dot-grid: absolute inset-0, opacity-40, pointer-events-none]
  <container-pro relative>
    <p overline text-white/45 mb-5> — section label
    <h1 text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-bold tracking-tighter text-white max-w-2xl>
```

- All page heroes use the same structure. Differentiate with photo choice, not layout variation.
- Overline label: `text-white/45` — always.
- Hero H1: `text-white`, never `text-foreground` (which resolves to near-white but avoids CSS variable confusion in hero context).

### Cards

**Standard card:**
```
bg-card border border-border rounded-xl p-8
hover:border-brand-red/30 hover:shadow-[var(--shadow-card)]
transition-all duration-300 ease-[cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)]
```

- Icon badges: `w-10 h-10 rounded-lg bg-brand-red/8 flex items-center justify-center`
- Icon fill: `text-brand-red`
- Card heading: `text-base font-semibold tracking-tight text-primary`... wait — `text-foreground` not `text-primary`
- Body text: `text-sm text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed`

**Featured cell (dark):**
```
bg-[hsl(var(--primary-deep))] text-white border border-white/5 p-10
```
- Used for the "People" bento cell, "Why Mobilise" card, info panels.
- Icon badge: `bg-white/12`

**Bento grid:**
```
grid md:grid-cols-2 gap-px bg-border rounded-2xl overflow-hidden
```
The `gap-px bg-border` trick: grid gap becomes the border color, children have `bg-card` or `bg-[hsl(var(--primary-deep))]` to fill their cells. Clean grid lines with no extra border CSS.

### Buttons

**Primary CTA (Signal Red):**
```
bg-brand-red hover:bg-brand-red-hover text-white font-semibold
px-6 py-3 rounded text-sm transition-colors whitespace-nowrap
btn-shine btn-tactile
style: box-shadow: var(--shadow-red)
```

**Secondary (ghost outline):**
```
border border-white/20 hover:border-white/40 text-white/80 hover:text-white
font-semibold px-6 py-3 rounded text-sm transition-all duration-300
btn-shine btn-tactile
```

**Tab / underline:**
```
pb-4 text-sm font-semibold border-b-2 -mb-px transition-colors min-h-[44px]
active: border-brand-red text-foreground
inactive: border-transparent text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground
```

- Minimum touch target: 44px (`min-h-[44px]`). Applied to all interactive elements.
- Never use `text-primary` (Survey Blue) for button label text — use `text-white` on colored backgrounds.

### Overline / section labels

```
<p class="overline">  — CSS utility class
```
Defined in `index.css`: 10px, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.35em, `text-muted-foreground`.
Used as the eyebrow above every major section heading.

### Stats / numbers

Large numbers communicating scale:
```
text-4xl font-bold tracking-tighter text-foreground leading-none mb-1
```
Label below:
```
text-sm text-muted-foreground font-medium
```
Never `text-primary` for stat numbers — they should read as `text-foreground` (white/light).

### Contact info panel

The dark information card on Contacts page:
```
bg-[hsl(var(--primary-deep))] text-white rounded-2xl p-8 lg:p-10 relative overflow-hidden
```
Status badge:
```
inline-flex items-center gap-2.5 bg-success/12 border border-success/25 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full
```

### Navigation

Header background adapts on scroll (transparent → `bg-[hsl(var(--background))/0.9] backdrop-blur-sm`).

Language switcher selected state:
```
bg-foreground/15 text-foreground
```
Never `bg-primary` — Survey Blue background with white text at 68% lightness fails contrast at small sizes.

---

## Do's and Don'ts

### Do

- Use `container-pro` for all page section containers (consistent horizontal padding + max-width).
- Wrap all content sections in `<RevealSection>` for scroll-triggered fade-in.
- Use `heading-underline` on H2 section headings to create the blueprint annotation aesthetic.
- Use `gap-px bg-border` grid trick for bento layouts — cleaner than individual cell borders.
- Maintain the 44px minimum touch target on all interactive elements.
- Use Signal Red only for icons, active states, accents, and CTAs. Not body text.
- Use photo heroes: every page hero has a full-bleed desaturated photo behind gradient overlays.
- Keep body text `max-w-[65ch]` — the drydock blueprint is precise, not sprawling.

### Don't

- **No side-stripe borders.** No `border-left` or `border-right` colored accents. Rewrite with background tints or nothing.
- **No gradient text** (`background-clip: text`). Solid colors only.
- **No `text-primary` on headings or stat numbers.** Survey Blue is for links and interactive text only. Headings use `text-foreground`.
- **No identical card grids.** Vary layout: bento, asymmetric columns, list rows, featured cells.
- **No marketing superlatives.** "World-class", "leading", "cutting-edge" — cut them. Certifications and named ports do the work.
- **No em dashes.** Use commas, colons, or periods instead.
- **No dynamic Tailwind class construction** (`\`group-hover:${variable}\``). Tailwind scans static strings only. Pre-build complete class strings in theme objects.
- **No `#000` or `#fff` raw.** Use the tinted neutrals.
- **No bounce or elastic easing.** Use `ease-[cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1)]` (expo-out) for all UI motion.
- **No hero-metric template** (big number + gradient accent). Stats live in sidebars and cards, not hero panels.
